compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Local dev / agent clone identity setup + docs

Each human/agent clone writes non-exempt tickets and needs its OWN identity (not the bot).

- Document the setup in `docs/identity.md` / `docs/local-dev-env.md`: create/own an identity ticket, `rebar identity use <id>` (writes git-ignored `.rebar/current_identity`), and set `identity.signing_key` (or export `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY`) to the developer's local SSH private key under `~/.ssh/`.
- Note the `git config user.email` fallback resolution path.
- Make explicit that the signing key is per-machine and never committed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/identity.md` (and/or `docs/local-dev-env.md`) documents the per-clone identity + signing-key setup and the git-email fallback.
- [ ] A local clone following the docs produces a signed write, verifiable via `rebar show <id>` (the written event shows `authorship.signed ≥ 1`) and/or `rebar verify-authorship` emitting a `verified` verdict for it. (NOT `verify-signature`, which certifies HMAC manifest attestations from the `rebar sign` flow, not SSH-based event authorship.)
- [ ] Docs state the key is per-machine and uncommitted.
compiled_state.title: Local dev / agent clone identity + signing-key setup + docs
